description: Linear pipeline definition. Each step references a spec_id from ui_spec.json. path: critical | optional | later.

path_legend.critical: Must complete before the pipeline can advance to the next stage.
path_legend.optional: Improves results or observability but the pipeline runs without it.
path_legend.later: Do after the first full pipeline cycle. Reduces review burden and increases confidence in future extractions.

stages.label: Track Config
stages.description: Define which companies to track and how the pipeline is configured. Steps here are prerequisites for ingestion.

steps.label: Add Companies
steps.description: Add at least one company. Provide a CIK for EDGAR filing access and an IR URL for press release scraping.
steps.note: Without a CIK the pipeline cannot acquire SEC filings. Without an IR URL the LLM crawl has no seed.

steps.label: Settings
steps.description: LLM provider and model, confidence thresholds, context window size, crawl limits.
steps.note: Defaults are production-ready. Adjust after seeing first extraction results if agreement rates are low.

steps.label: Crawl Setup
steps.description: Customize the LLM crawl prompt per ticker. The default prompt covers production_btc, hodl_btc, and sold_btc.
steps.note: Only needed if a company uses unusual terminology not covered by the default prompt.

stages.label: Ingest
stages.description: Fetch raw documents from EDGAR and IR press release sources. Nothing can be extracted until documents exist.

steps.label: EDGAR Acquire
steps.description: Pull SEC filings (8-K, 10-Q, 10-K, 20-F) for all tracked companies with a CIK.
steps.note: The earliest bitcoin mention date from EDGAR is automatically injected into the LLM crawl prompt as a lower bound. Run EDGAR first.

steps.label: LLM Crawl (IR press releases)
steps.description: LLM-directed crawl of IR press release pages. Supplements EDGAR with monthly production announcements that do not appear in filings.
steps.note: Monthly press releases often contain the most precise production figures. EDGAR 10-Q/10-K filings cover quarterly data only.

steps.label: Ingest Status
steps.description: Pipeline observability: document counts, extraction backlog, config health per company.
steps.note: Check here if extraction results are unexpectedly sparse — it may indicate a scraper config problem.

stages.label: Interpret
stages.description: Run LLM + regex extraction on all ingested documents. The LLM runs unconditionally; regex provides corroboration.

steps.label: Extract
steps.description: LLM extracts values from every stored document. When LLM and regex agree, the result auto-accepts to data_points. When only LLM fires, the item routes to Review Queue for analyst decision.
steps.note: No regex patterns are required to run extraction. Patterns only increase the auto-accept rate.

steps.label: QC Snapshot
steps.description: Capture a pipeline health snapshot: agreement rate, review queue depth, extraction coverage per company.
steps.note: Take a snapshot before and after adding regex patterns to measure the improvement in auto-accept rate.

stages.label: Review
stages.description: Analyst approves or rejects LLM-only extraction candidates. Approved items enter data_points.

steps.label: Review Queue
steps.description: Approve or reject each LLM-only candidate. A rejected item is excluded permanently. Undecided items stay pending.
steps.note: Use J/K to navigate, A to approve, R to reject. Bulk approve is available for high-confidence batches.

steps.label: Build Patterns (feedback loop)
steps.description: After approving at least 10-20 items, use the Gen button on any approved row to auto-generate a regex from that snippet. Save it to the library. On the next extraction run, the pattern corroborates the LLM and the item auto-accepts without review.
steps.note: This step closes the feedback loop. Each pattern you save reduces future review volume for that phrase. Aim to cover the 5-10 most common document formats per company before the next extraction cycle.

steps.label: Tune Metric Rules (thresholds + valid ranges)
steps.description: After observing real extraction results, adjust agreement thresholds and outlier detection ranges per metric. Default values are conservative and safe for a first run.
steps.note: Only change these if you see correct values being flagged as outliers, or if incorrect values are auto-accepting. Requires at least 6 months of data to tune meaningfully.

stages.label: Data
stages.description: Explore and validate the verified dataset. Identify coverage gaps and trigger re-extraction.

steps.label: Coverage Explorer
steps.description: Heatmap showing data coverage by company and month. Seven states: data present, gap sentinel, pending review, no document, low confidence, analyst edit, quarterly.
steps.note: Grey cells mean no document was ingested for that period. White cells mean a document exists but extraction found nothing. These require different remediation.

steps.label: Document Registry
steps.description: Browse all ingested documents with source type, parse quality, and extraction status. Filter by ticker, period, or source type.
steps.note: Use this to diagnose parse failures (e.g. PDF that failed to render) or to identify documents that were ingested but never extracted.

stages.label: Interrogate
stages.description: Natural language queries and analysis against the verified dataset.

steps.label: Interrogate
steps.description: LLM-powered time-series analysis. Ask questions directly against the extracted dataset.
steps.note: Requires sufficient data coverage before results are meaningful. Recommended minimum: 12 months of data for at least 5 companies.
